methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

data_flows.source: ETH donations via Treasury to Tokenomics.trackServiceDonations
path: Treasury calls trackServiceDonations with donator, serviceIds, amounts, donationETH
_trackServiceDonations splits amounts across service units
mapUnitIncentives accumulates pendingRelativeReward/pendingRelativeTopUp per unit
mapEpochTokenomics accumulates totalDonationsETH, sumUnitTopUpsOLAS
checkpoint() finalizes epoch: calculates IDF, splits incentives into treasury/component/agent rewards
accountOwnerIncentives finalizes pending incentives and returns reward/topUp
Dispenser.claimOwnerIncentives calls Treasury.withdrawToAccount
data_flows.trust_changes: Treasury is trusted caller; external registry calls for service/unit validation
validation_points: veOLAS threshold check for top-up eligibility
exposure_risks: Truncation in amount/numServiceUnits loses ETH allocation to units
pendingRelativeReward/topUp stored as uint96 could overflow with massive donations
manipulation_risks: Flash loan to inflate veOLAS for top-up eligibility (mitigated by same-block check)
Creating many-unit services to dilute per-unit rewards

path: checkpoint() calculates inflationPerEpoch from inflationPerSecond * epoch duration
Year-change logic splits inflation across two year periods
inflationPerEpoch split into: maxBond fraction, component/agent topUp fractions, staking fraction
effectiveBond updated with maxBond delta
mapEpochStakingPoints[eCounter].stakingIncentive accumulates staking allocation + refunds
Dispenser.calculateStakingIncentives reads stakingIncentive and distributes per voting weight
transformations: Split across year boundary: partial_year1 + partial_year2
data_flows.trust_changes: Anyone can call checkpoint; epoch timing is permissionless
validation_points: Epoch length check: diffNumSeconds >= epochLen && <= MAX_EPOCH_LENGTH
exposure_risks: Delayed checkpoint causes inflation undercount for passing year
Year skip scenario with >1 year gap (mitigated by MAX_EPOCH_LENGTH < ONE_YEAR)
manipulation_risks: Timing checkpoint to maximize/minimize inflation per epoch around year boundaries
Delaying checkpoint to extend epoch and get more inflation (bounded by MAX_EPOCH_LENGTH)

path: User calls claimStakingIncentives/claimStakingIncentivesBatch
calculateStakingIncentives loops over epochs, reads VoteWeighting for nominee relative weight
Compares with minStakingWeight threshold; adjusts by maxStakingAmount cap
Accounts for bridging decimals normalization
Deducts withheld amounts from transfer
Mints OLAS via Treasury.withdrawToAccount
Distributes to L2 via deposit processor bridge
data_flows.trust_changes: Anyone can trigger claim; deposit processor must be correctly configured by owner
validation_points: Nominee existence and epoch range validation
Ascending order check for batch targets
exposure_risks: Bridge failure can cause OLAS to be stuck in deposit processor
Withheld amount sync failure between L1 and L2
manipulation_risks: Claiming for removed nominees up to removal epoch
Repeatedly calling calculateStakingIncentives (public function) triggers checkpointNominee

path: _receiveMessage validates relayer and source processor
_processData decodes targets and amounts
For each target: stakingFactory.verifyInstanceAndGetEmissionsAmount (low-level call)
Valid targets receive OLAS via approve+deposit
Invalid/excess amounts withheld locally
Insufficient balance causes queueing for later redeem
transformations: Amount capped by limitAmount from factory verification
withheldAmount accumulated for sync back to L1
data_flows.trust_changes: Bridge relayer and L1 deposit processor are trusted origins
validation_points: Staking factory verification (low-level call, never reverts)
exposure_risks: Low-level call to stakingFactory means verification failure is silent
Queued items can be redeemed without re-verification of staking target
manipulation_risks: Owner can call processDataMaintenance with arbitrary data
Staking target could become invalid between queuing and redeeming

path: User calls stake(serviceId) - transfers NFT service to contract
Checkpoint called periodically - calculates rewards based on liveness
ActivityChecker.getMultisigNonces and isRatioPass determine eligibility
Rewards accumulated in mapServiceInfo[serviceId].reward
Inactivity tracked and leads to eviction if exceeding threshold
User unstakes - service returned, rewards withdrawn
transformations: If totalRewards > availableRewards: proportional scaling with dust to first service
data_flows.trust_changes: ActivityChecker is trusted external contract (low-level call pattern)
validation_points: Agent IDs, config hash, threshold checks
exposure_risks: ActivityChecker failure (returns false by default) treats service as inactive
Reward dust systematically favors first service in array
manipulation_risks: Staking right before checkpoint to get time-weighted reward
Unstaking right after checkpoint to extract accumulated rewards

path: User calls voteForNomineeWeights with account, chainId, weight
Reads user's veOLAS slope and lockEnd
Calculates new slope based on weight allocation (weight/10000 of user slope)
Updates pointsWeight for nominee and pointsSum for all nominees
Schedules slope changes at lock expiration times
Weight used in Dispenser.calculateStakingIncentives via nomineeRelativeWeight
data_flows.trust_changes: Permissionless voting; veOLAS data is trusted from VotingEscrow
exposure_risks: Removed nominees' future slope changes still affect changesSum if not revoked
MAX_NUM_WEEKS (53) cap means >1 year gap prevents full checkpoint catch-up
manipulation_risks: Strategic voting just before epoch end to maximize weight for specific target
Not revoking votes on removed nominees to distort total weight sum

core_rules: Epoch-based tokenomics: rewards and inflation distributed per epoch
Inflation per second derived from yearly inflation schedule, adjusted at year boundaries
effectiveBond = accumulated maxBond credits minus reserved bond programs
Staking incentives capped by: (1) total veOLAS directed to staking, (2) per-nominee maxStakingAmount, (3) minStakingWeight threshold
Service staking requires minimum deposit, deployed state, correct multisig proxy hash
Activity verification via external checker with configurable liveness ratio
economic_incentives: ETH donations to services generate rewards for component/agent owners
veOLAS holders above threshold qualify service owners for OLAS top-ups
OLAS inflation funds bonding programs, developer top-ups, and staking incentives
Staking rewards proportional to time staked and activity level (rewardsPerSecond * ts)
Voting weight determines staking incentive allocation per nominee
Retainer mechanism returns unallocated staking incentives to inflation
governance_mechanisms: Owner controls: parameter changes (deferred to next epoch), manager addresses, registries
DAO/Timelock controls: pause state, staking params, maintenance functions
Permissionless: checkpoint, nominee addition, staking/unstaking, claiming
Parameter changes take effect at next epoch via flag-based deferred update system

edge_cases.scenario: Tokenomics.refundFromStaking error message references wrong address (depository instead of dispenser)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Error should show dispenser address as the required manager
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Error message shows depository address: revert ManagerOnly(msg.sender, depository)
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No direct exploit but misleading error information for debugging

edge_cases.scenario: EthereumDepositProcessor._deposit reverts entire batch if any target has zero limitAmount
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Graceful handling like L2 dispensers (withhold and continue)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: revert TargetEmissionsZero(target) blocks all targets in batch
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A single invalid/disabled staking target on L1 blocks incentive distribution for all L1 targets in the same batch; could be weaponized by front-running disablement of a staking instance

edge_cases.scenario: Donation amount split across service units truncates to zero for small amounts
edge_cases.expected_behavior: All donated ETH allocated proportionally to units
edge_cases.actual_behavior: uint96(amounts[i] / numServiceUnits) truncates; e.g., 99 wei across 100 units = 0 per unit; 99 wei lost from unit reward allocation
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Negligible amounts lost per truncation; systematically favors services with fewer units

edge_cases.scenario: StakingBase checkpoint reward dust allocation to first eligible service
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Equal proportional distribution when totalRewards > availableRewards
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Loop starts from i=1, processes service[0] last and assigns rounding dust to it
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: First service in setServiceIds systematically receives a few extra wei; order depends on staking sequence and eviction history

edge_cases.scenario: Tokenomics.checkpoint has no reentrancy guard despite external call to Treasury.rebalanceTreasury
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Reentrancy should be prevented on checkpoint
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _locked variable exists but is not checked in checkpoint(). External call to treasury occurs before epochCounter increment.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If treasury contract is compromised/malicious, reentrant call to checkpoint could double-process epoch data. Mitigated by: (1) treasury is owner-set trusted contract, (2) same block.timestamp means epoch time check would pass identically leading to state corruption

edge_cases.scenario: VoteWeighting.removeNominee does not zero slope or clean up changesWeight/changesSum for future timestamps
edge_cases.expected_behavior: All weight-related data for removed nominee cleaned up
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only bias at nextTime is zeroed. Future slope changes in changesSum remain unless users call revokeRemovedNomineeVotingPower
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If users do not revoke voting power from removed nominees, changesSum will be incorrectly decremented at lock expiration times, reducing total weight sum and increasing relative weight of remaining nominees

edge_cases.scenario: DefaultTargetDispenserL2.redeem does not re-verify staking target validity
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Target validity should be checked at redemption time
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only checks that queueHash exists; no re-verification against stakingFactory
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A staking target disabled after queuing can still receive OLAS through redeem; the amount was already adjusted at queue time but contract validity may have changed

edge_cases.scenario: StakingFactory.setInstanceStatus only allows deployer to change status
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Factory owner should be able to disable malicious instances
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only the original deployer can enable/disable an instance; factory owner has no override
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A malicious deployer can create an instance that passes initial verification but later behaves maliciously, and the factory owner cannot disable it

edge_cases.scenario: Dispenser.calculateStakingIncentives compares stakingIncentive (OLAS) with totalWeightSum (veOLAS bias units)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Comparison between amounts in same units
edge_cases.actual_behavior: availableStakingAmount (OLAS inflation amount) compared with totalWeightSum (veOLAS bias/voting power). Units are fundamentally different
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: By design: caps staking distribution by total veOLAS directed to staking. Low veOLAS participation means less staking incentive distributed regardless of allocated OLAS amount. Not exploitable but could lead to underdistribution of staking incentives

edge_cases.scenario: Dispenser.calculateStakingIncentives is a public function that modifies external state
edge_cases.expected_behavior: View-only calculation or protected by access control
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Calls IVoteWeighting.checkpointNominee which modifies state; anyone can call to checkpoint nominees at no cost beyond gas
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Anyone can trigger nominee checkpoint; the doc says to use staticcall but there is no enforcement. Could be used to keep nominees checkpointed for free

edge_cases.scenario: Tokenomics._finalizeIncentivesForUnitId top-up calculation can result in zero for small contributions
edge_cases.actual_behavior: topUp = (pendingRelativeTopUp * totalTopUpsOLAS * topUpUnitFraction) / (100 * sumUnitTopUpsOLAS). With small pendingRelativeTopUp relative to sumUnitTopUpsOLAS, result truncates to zero
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Small contributors lose their top-up allocation to truncation. Large contributors systematically benefit from accumulated rounding in their favor

edge_cases.scenario: Dispenser.claimOwnerIncentives reverts if reward and topUp are both zero
edge_cases.actual_behavior: success remains false when (reward + topUp) == 0, causing revert ClaimIncentivesFailed
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Not exploitable; just bad UX. Users calling with no accumulated incentives get a confusing revert

edge_cases.scenario: Year boundary inflation split assumes at most one year change per epoch
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Handle multiple year changes if epoch spans more than one year
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only calculates inflation for two year periods. MAX_EPOCH_LENGTH < ONE_YEAR prevents >1 year epochs
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Protected by MAX_EPOCH_LENGTH constraint. If constraint were removed, inflation calculation would be incorrect for multi-year epochs

assumption_violations: Treasury rebalanceTreasury is assumed to not re-enter tokenomics; no explicit reentrancy guard on checkpoint
Bridge message delivery is assumed reliable; failure requires DAO intervention via maintenance functions
All users are assumed to eventually revoke votes from removed nominees; unrevoked votes distort weight calculations
ActivityChecker is assumed to be correctly implemented; failures default to inactive (safe default)
StakingFactory deployers are assumed to be non-malicious for instance management; owner cannot override
Service registry ownerOf is assumed to return correct owners; compromised registry breaks reward distribution
Block.timestamp is assumed to be reasonably accurate; miners can manipulate by ~15 seconds (negligible impact)
game_theory_risks: Services with fewer units receive proportionally more reward per unit from donations (inversely proportional to numServiceUnits)
Strategic checkpoint timing: delaying checkpoint accumulates more inflation per epoch, benefiting current epoch recipients
Vote weight manipulation: concentrating votes on specific nominees just before epoch end to maximize their allocation
Staking sandwich: staking immediately before checkpoint and unstaking after to capture disproportionate time-weighted rewards
Retainer voting power can be used to reduce staking incentive distribution, returning funds to inflation pool
Cross-chain arbitrage: withheld amounts on L2 offset future L1 transfers, creating credit that could be exploited if sync timing is manipulated
integration_risks: Five different bridge implementations with varying failure modes and recovery mechanisms
Wormhole bridge has 8-decimal precision limit, causing truncation loss on each transfer
Bridge message failures require DAO votes for maintenance recovery (processDataMaintenance, syncWithheldAmountMaintenance)
VoteWeighting MAX_NUM_WEEKS=53 means checkpoints must happen within ~1 year to avoid stale weights
External ActivityChecker contract failures (revert or wrong data) treated as inactive service, possibly causing unjust eviction
Polygon FxPortal requires separate fxChildTunnel/fxRootTunnel setup that must be consistent with l2TargetDispenser
Arbitrum address aliasing must be correctly computed for L1->L2 message validation
calculation_errors: Tokenomics.refundFromStaking error message uses depository address instead of dispenser: revert ManagerOnly(msg.sender, depository) should be revert ManagerOnly(msg.sender, dispenser)
Donation amount truncation: uint96(amounts[i] / numServiceUnits) loses remainder that is still counted in totalDonationsETH but never distributed to units
Bridge decimal normalization can lose up to 10^(18-bridgingDecimals)-1 tokens per transfer (e.g., up to ~10^10 wei for Wormhole with 8 decimals)

token_semantics.notes: Standard ERC20 with inflation schedule. Initial 1B cap for 10 years, then 2% annual inflation. Minted via Treasury. Used as staking deposit token and incentive distribution token across chains.
Vote-escrowed OLAS. Provides voting power for VoteWeighting gauge. Slope/bias model. Used for top-up eligibility threshold checks in Tokenomics.

invariants.name: effective_bond_accounting
invariants.rule: effectiveBond = accumulated maxBond credits - reserved bond programs + leftovers

invariants.name: staking_rewards_conservation
invariants.rule: balance >= availableRewards + sum(mapServiceInfo[*].reward) for all staked services

invariants.name: epoch_monotonicity
invariants.rule: endTime strictly increasing across epochs; epochCounter only increments by 1

invariants.name: claimed_epoch_monotonicity
invariants.rule: mapLastClaimedStakingEpochs[nomineeHash] only increases or stays the same

invariants.name: withheld_amount_conservation
invariants.rule: L2 withheldAmount should eventually sync to L1 mapChainIdWithheldAmounts and offset future transfers
